reviews.review_description: I chose Russian little ￼ did I know ￼ Russian is hard it said  “ ￼ change language whenever you want “ the maker of the game is a LIAR, I WANTED TO CHANGE MY LANGUAGE AND IT WOULDN’T LET ME I KEPT ASKING HOW DO I CHANGE MY LANGUAGE AND IT SAID GO TO THE SETTINGS OR MAIN MENU THERE IS NO SETTINGS OR MAIN MENU. PLEASE FIX THIS ￼￼￼￼

developer_response.body:
Hi, this is not a bug.

You can change the language after the tutorial ends. At any time you can aslo go to the QUEST SETTINGS MENU (not the in-game settings menu) then SETTINGS > GENERAL > STORAGE, then click the "Delete data" icon next to Noun Town (not uninstall) and you can pick your language again.

It's really disappointing you're leaving a negative review for something that isn't a bug. Saying we're [a] "LIAR" is also pretty rude.

Can you try that and update us here please. Thank you

reviews.review_title: Solid game if they fix the bugs
reviews.review_description: It's a solid game to help learn a language but I had a few bugs most I could get around. The main one was for Japanese the console in game to learn numbers from the robot stopped working. So I could only learn up to 20. I'll change to 5 star if that gets fixed because it's a pretty solid addition to your studies
I've used this app for a long time without problems, but now it won't pick-up my speech.  It won't let me report the problem.

I wanted to learn korean but once you go into the game you cant hear anything or do anything, it seemed fun but cant really do anything, idk if i need to play it more or what but always read reviews first before you buy or get anything. I hope this helped!

developer_response.body:
Hello, this sounds like something is up with your setup as we have thousands of players playing the game successfully, including Korean (we've verified it is working ourselves, too).

On your Quest headset, go to SETTINGS > GENERAL > STORAGE, then click the "Delete data" icon next to Noun Town (not uninstall)

It's disappointing you've left a review without having experienced the game, hopefully the above step can resolve your settings issue for you.

Please can you update us here when you have followed the step above. Thank you

reviews.review_description: I absolutely love this little game, it's super interactive and engaging. There's something special here and I  think if they keep on working on it, it'll be a huge hit. (Also dont think i didn't notice a suspiciously familiar green bird dead by the boat 🤣) Super funny touch and just goes to show the developers know how to make a joke. I'm currently taking the MX spanish route as i go down south a lot and i love how easy it is to repeat and perfect everything. The speech feature is really nice, helping me with pronunciation.

I love the idea of this and it has amazing potential. Unfortunately there are so many glitches the app is mostly unusable. Some games don't work, instructions are lacking, and general glitches in gameplay like holding items and pushing buttons work only sometimes. To developers - please work on fixing these bugs! This could be such an amazing app!

developer_response.body:
Hello, I'm sorry you're not getting on with the game. This is not typical of the thousands of users we have playing the game successfully. Holding items, pushing buttons, and playing the mini-games successfully happens thousands of times a day from our Quest users, so it seems something isn't right on your specific setup (we also test the game on all Quest devices regularly and have found no issues). There's also a comprehensive onboarding process and set of tooltips throughout the game - perhaps you missed these.

If you reach out to contact@noun.town we can help you to enjoy the game as thousands of our other users are. Also, to claim the app is "mostly unusable" is misleading to others.

reviews.review_description:
Great for learning vocabulary in desired language.
And you may be able to learn sentence structure by talking to NPCs and translating their message. 
This game most likely will not teach you your desired language.

Some of the games that help you practice known vocabulary are great and genuinely fun.

The voice recognition is alright, not great.

Moves way to quick and I can’t understand a thing when I started up the app for the fist time it literally was trying to talk to me in hangul (Korean alphabet) which I couldn’t understand and the pace is very off. Also, the game is just very buggy and it’s honestly just a mess. Don’t recommend.

I had a 23 day streak and one day while I was playing the game it froze and my whole headset shut off. My headset is barely a month old. When I opened the game back all my progress was gone. I literally cannot get past language selection. Whenever it asks me what language I speak I tap English and nothing happens. I have deleted and redownloaded it multiple times and I still can’t get past language selection. I can handle glitches but no other apps give me issues like this. I’m still in month 1 of my horizon trial so I’d have been really upset if I paid for this game and it wasn’t working. I’d like to continue playing if there’s a solution.

developer_response.body:
Hi tapjump, 

I'm sorry to hear this. If you clear your save file the game should work again. Here's how to do that:

On your Quest headset, go to SETTINGS > GENERAL > STORAGE, then click the "Delete data" icon next to Noun Town (not uninstall)

This will mean you lose your progress - I'm sorry about that, it seems something got corrupted in your save file.

Please can you try the process above and update us back on here on how you get on?

Thank you

reviews.review_description: This language system is inmersing , just like we started young learning, focusing on food and things even if we into say them right then get the rest from school
There's always some listening problem with the mic when using this app .  I need to repeat the words again and again.
Please consider adding European Portuguese (from Portugal) so I can use the app 🥺

reviews.review_title: I LOOOOVEEE THIS APP! The Best one!
reviews.review_description: For me its the best app lenguage that Ive ever seen. Thank you soo much.

reviews.review_title: where to find the clam in noun town vr
reviews.review_description: I can’t find the clam on the beach and there was something else missing in the bakery. It’s a little bit frustrating to try to learn everything else since when it pops up as a daily work you won’t be able to complete the rest of the items.
Noun Town Language Learning is so limited on languages. I mean com’on you would think, Vietnamese or Thai language would be added by now, but nope.

Really like the idea of the game. And it does excel in certain aspects of learning. I tried learning the russian. Some of the objects i had no idea what they were. It would help to have words of the item in your native language in text above or below the item. Also, it doesn't teach you how to pronounce any specific words/ sounds / letters. You nust get thrown right into some of the words are not beginner friendly at all. Great idea, but it seems to need some fine tuning.

reviews.review_title: Loved the Virtual Airship – Please Bring
reviews.review_description: Hi, I’ve been using Noun Town for almost three years on my Quest 2. Mixed reality isn’t in colour on Quest 2, so it looks really messy. I really miss when the airship was fully virtual. It was way more immersive and easier to use. Can you please bring that feature back? I really want to know if it’s possible.
So much games

reviews.review_title: Working again after deleting my save
reviews.review_description: Can no longer start practice mode, or learn new objects when entering a room the display cabinets remains blank. It was working fine up until now

developer_response.body: Hi callumo1, thank you so much for letting us know it's working again :) Super appreciated!

reviews.review_description: The first day I got it the app worked perfectly fine besides a few microphone issues. The second day I tried logging on but when I got on all of the language options were grey and I couldn’t even click anything now it is just stuck on the starting screen saying noun town which is very frustrated especially because I paid money for this game when I could’ve gotten something else…

developer_response.body: Hello, sorry to hear this - sounds like an issue with install / update. This can be resolved by deleting the Noun Town save file -> on your Quest headset, go to SETTINGS > GENERAL > STORAGE, then click the "Delete data" icon next to Noun Town (not uninstall). Please can you update us if that works, if not we can help more. Thank you.

reviews.review_description: I tried this out shortly after it first came out and I was rather disappointed, everything seemed slow and glitched and didn't really have a desire to continue. However, after the January update, the game is now actually amazing. It feels like a game, and after some time, I can just start learning Japanese in a flow state while being immersed in the Japanese language. The game only has around 1000 words so it's a great way to start some fundimental learning while playing a game. It also has potential for major expansion in the future as you can see places for new shops to come up or other games. I am really impressed by the new experience, and I hope the game gets the attention it deserves and continues to grow. Well done.
Great looking game and makes me excited to learn but the game doesn’t work. None of the locations work and character interactions are buggy. Wish they’d fix it

developer_response.body: Hello, how do you mean the locations don't work? Also, what is the issue with the character interactions? Please can you explain so we can understand what's going wrong? Thank you

reviews.review_description:
I'm currently in the early stages of (finally) learning Spanish and it seemed like a great time to supplement my learning with this app. However, things didn't go as planned...

I don't want to play this game in MR. I never played it before in MR. I want to play in VR. I had not opened this app in a very long time and since then, MR support was added. When I opened it, I was told that I *MUST* enable "allow passthrough" for the app to run in MR. Why?! I don't want to run in MR, but fine... I dug through the settings and enabled that.

Then I go to change it to VR, but nothing actually changes. It supposedly only applies on launch. So I close the app and reopen. It is still stuck in MR mode. It doesn't matter what mode my headset is in when I enter the game. I tried both passthrough and non-passthrough. Frustrating...

So I proceed to get past whatever initial lesson there is. I am told to pick up some boxes, but they are not in my play space and are out of reach (even with the grab ray thing). Trying to walk outside of my boundary causes the game to fade out, so I cannot pick them up to proceed.

Also, if you change the language from your native language, it disappears from the list and you cannot get back to it. At first, I thought I was forced to select another language then re-enter settings, but I guess you can just close out and that setting will be ignored. What a weird design decision/omission.

Reinstalling didn't help. This pain did lead me to another app (IMMERSE) which I will try instead. Sorry, devs. I wish you the best of luck and hope you get this sorted.

I would have thought after being out for 3+ years, stuff like this would be sorted.

developer_response.body:
Hi, sorry about this - it sounds like as you have skipped a few updates something has gone wrong (as we, and Meta, have released many major updates in that time). If you clear your save file it'll start over and you can choose to stay in VR (you will lose your historic progress though): 

On your Quest headset, go to Settings > Storage, then click the "Delete data" icon next to Noun Town (not uninstall)

Please let us know if that helps. Thank you.

reviews.review_description: This is by far the most frustrating and unintuitive game I’ve played on the Meta Quest. The instructions are few and unhelpful. I found myself doing trial and error—repeatedly pushing all the buttons to get something to work. The in-game menu button which did something useful on every other Meta Quest game I tried, did nothing. And after identifying the 5 objects, and coloring them in with voice recognition, I couldn’t find anyway to make them “learned”, or move on. I also couldn’t find anyway to move between the various locations that are supposed to be part of this game.

developer_response.body:
Hello, it sounds like you've missed the tutorial, to re-trigger that follow these steps: on your Quest headset, go to Settings > Storage, then click the "Delete data" icon next to Noun Town (not uninstall). The tutorial has 25+ prompts that explain how the game works, including the process for testing the words in the kiosk in each room. 
The B button brings up the in-game menu.

Thank you

reviews.review_description: I would love to be able to give this game a 5 stars. The concept is great and when its all working properly ai enjoy my time learning new words. Plus,  I love that its a one time fee instead of subscription. However , I have had multiple glitchy game play experiences where I can only play the punch game 2x or my mic was working fine and then all of a sudden its constantly giving me a "mic error, heard nothing, is your mic on?" message. The controls are also clunky and glitchy. Many times I can not point at objects to hear and see the words or just struggle picking up an item. As some have mentioned the instructions are also lacking. I see adjectives and verbs in the menu but don't really know how to use them/access an action with them.  I would also like to understand the testing better. Overall, it is good and worth $20, if it had a few improvements easily 5 stars.

I was searching for an app that can help grow my vocabulary in Spanish in a fun way. I have a good foundation in Spanish but I need to build it up and practice. This was the app I needed.

It was so much fun “running around” looking for the items. The tutorial to begin the game is a must and easy to follow.  

I played for 15 minutes and I already learned a new word in my target language.

I’m really happy with this app. I’d love to see a similar app called verb town and description town. Each app could move on, but then I suppose it’d be like pictionary. 

In any case, a fun way to start building your vocab, refreshing it, and having fun doing it too. Great job devs!

reviews.review_title: Buggy and Should improve user interface
reviews.review_description:
I bought this app and kinda regretting.

1. After completed 5 items a day, there were no more 5 new items for the next day.

2. It says spanish with spanish flag but actually it is Mexican Spanish 

3. There is no guidance whatsoever. Like where to go for new tasks. 

4. The user interface is not kind enough for users

developer_response.body:
Hi xolacido, 

After you've learnt 5 words and tested them on the kiosk there's a "Learn more" button on the kiosk, when you press that you can learn more words in the same area (cafe for example). You can also, at any time, move to a new area to learn 5 more words (bakery, school, etc). So with the combination of the Learn more button, and moving to new areas, you can learn as much or as little as you like in one playing session.

The game offers Spanish (Spanish) AND Spanish (Mexican) you can change between them in the settings menu at any time.

The guidance is via the tutorial at the start and tool tips that pop up throughout the game - if there's anything specific you want to know, you can also email us at contact@noun.town.

Thank you.

---

Just checking back in - thanks for emailing us - did you receive our email with further support? Thanks

reviews.review_description:
This is the most fun I have had learning Japanese since I started. It feels very immersive, the characters are cute and it is satisfying to slowly colour in the world. It will not be great for learning grammar (at least so far) but for vocab I think it has been the best I've seen.

I really like the small lessons in each room where they show a scenario and you can act out as one of the characters, I would love to have more of those. Same with the opportunity to practice a verb by doing it, I wish there was an option to replay those little scenes in each room. Outside of that my only real criticism is that when you chat with the characters, the level of grammar that they use is often too difficult for beginners and there is no explanation which especially is tough for japanese since the english translation is usually not one-to-one and you won't learn a lot from that. 

However overall I would recommend this game to anyone, even if you have already been learning vocab for some time this will be a great reinforcement. Thank you to the developers for making such a great tool for learning.

I wanna start by saying the new island is *GORGEOUS*, and I love the tweaks and improvements, it’s given it a breath of fresh air and it’s looks fantastic. (I’m also hoping the blocked off areas with the construction style blocks are gonna be new areas in the future)
I’m using this in tandem with Duolingo to learn German and it’s really helping me to not only add extra words to my vocabulary that I wouldn’t get with Duo (sorry Duo) but the lessons help with applying that knowledge in reality in an alternative way that Duolingo doesn’t do/cover.
I enjoy the mini games and I feel like my learning has been greatly improved with this app, great job guys!

This game is actually a 8/10 (10/10 if I can learn conjugation) but the speech option does not work. It is so frustration that I don't want to Play/learn. Had a Spaniard try ku so it's not my pronounciation.

Plz help and it will be a 5 star review.

developer_response.body:
Hi, sorry for any frustration. What is the error message you're seeing when you try to record a word? Could you please try to clear your save and start over? Here's how to do that: on your Quest headset, go to Settings > Storage then click the "Delete data" icon next to Noun Town (not uninstall).

We are able to help further on contact@noun.town and discord too: https://discord.gg/3K3jWtCy3H Thank you

Hi, just checking back to see if this resolved your issue? Let us know! Thank you

reviews.review_description:
This seems like a great concept, but all I seem to have (in English to Ukrainian mode, anyway) is a never ending tutorial which teaches me nothing, and I can occasionally pick up a cat or a dog.

Feels like I'm missing something. I can't open any in-game menus for help, either.

developer_response.body:
Hi notciaranwest - sorry you're facing an issue - the tutorial takes about 5 mins and ends when you've learnt & tested five words at the kiosk in the cafe, so it seems like you've got stuck somewhere. I'd suggest clearing your save and starting over: on your Quest headset, go to Settings > Storage, then click the "Delete data" icon next to Noun Town.

If you'd like us to walk you through it step by step we can give one to one support via contact@noun.town or via our Discord: https://discord.gg/3K3jWtCy3H

Thank you.

reviews.review_description: This game is so fun and the concept is truly original. My complaints are that the kanji characters don't have furigana, and the conversations with NPCs are difficult to grasp for a beginner.

But annoying big when doing the find word excersise. When it does 2 the same words at a row (saying the word) it is actually using another word then given and you just have to guess till you find it. Happensultiple times within a find word session and messes up the experience..devs please fix this bug!

developer_response.body: Hey Mvoosten, thanks for the review! I want to fix this bug! Would you be able to share some more info on our Discord or send a message via Help (bottom right) on our website (https://noun.town/)? I hate bugs and want them all squashed [from Jack]

reviews.review_title: It kept breaking and getting stuck

developer_response.body:
Hi gideon.rubin.1, when you complete the tutorial the main menu unlocks and allows you to change language. 

You can also clear your save to start over and choose a new language: on your Quest headset, go to Settings > Storage, then click the "Delete data" icon next to Noun Town (not uninstall)

If you have more questions please email contact@noun.town or join our Discord where we can help more: https://discord.gg/3K3jWtCy3H

Did that help to resolve your problem??

learning Italian was fun in mixed reality? gotta go play it in the vr mode now

reviews.review_description: Really enjoying the interactivity of noun town and the mixed reality feature works great! If you're even remotely interested in learning the languages provided, it's a must buy.

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to review Noun Town, super appreciated and glad you like it!

reviews.review_description: What a great way to study a language it's interactive and interesting well made the voice recognition feels like magic and having 4 different voice recordings for each object is great been able to hear it from a male and female perspective really helps with the pronunciation this alone to expand your vocabulary would be worth it but it's so much more that with the lessons you learn real world ways of using the language so far I've only tried Japanese and just for this alone it's a 5 star game

They could not hear my voice!
They would use the wrong words that I had learned from "Drops Language Learning App"!
Some objects that I would not dare to say and use, such as, "Tabako"! 
"You have got me going dumb".
Refund!

developer_response.body: Hi Alec, I'm sorry to hear the speech recognition wasn't picking up your voice very well. Did you try using a Japanese accent when talking? We think it is useful for people to know many words, even ones they would not use. Personally, I would like to be able recognise the word, so that if someone offered me "tabako", I could say no! [from Jack]

reviews.review_description: for japanese in particular, kanji is displayed without furigana to guide new learners. And as other users point out, the voice recognition is super wonky. I will refund this

developer_response.body: Hi Kott95, sorry you didn't get on with the voice recognition. As for Japanese, you are also able to swap it with romaji in-context to guide a new learner (although it is true furigana is not displayed; it is very challenging to read small hiragana on a Quest screen)

reviews.review_description: I am someone who loves to learn languages. And when I saw this game I knew that I had to get it as soon as it came out , however when I went into the game to check it out, on a language that I'm already familiar with (Chinese). Instead of starting off with the basics such as 你好 (hello) or 你好吗（how are you) it instantly went to learning how to say chair, bread, cup, etc. which doesn't help a new learner. And especially for a language like Chinese, Pinyin (English pronunciation) is a very important thing if you would like to learn a language, and they never explain how it works with all the accents that you need to know to read Pinyin properly. This is a great game for practicing a language, not learning a completely new language.

developer_response.body:
Hi Danny, thanks for buying, playing + reviewing.

We made Noun Town for people who find traditional language learning methods difficult or ineffective, so the format will be quite different to textbook learning. As you can see from a lot of the other reviews - this method really resonates with many learners!

Regarding GREETINGS, we teach them in one of our early lessons (of which there are 75 lessons in the game. Did you unlock them yet? You gain access to context-specific lessons as you progress in each space).

We also have (when unlocked) every character greet you, with different words depending on the day and night. They can also give slightly different greetings depending on their personalities - just like real people!

reviews.review_description: I was having several issues with this app but the team got right on it and corrected my issues. It turns out that the app needed to be reinstalled and the VR headset needed an upgrade. Thanks for the quick response.

developer_response.body: Hi GhotiManVideo, thanks for sticking with us to get the issues sorted and for the awesome review. It's really appreciated :)

reviews.review_description: I was so looking forward to this app and expecting this app to be good, but I really didn't think it would have so many bugs. 1st. It's so jittery and looks like it's dropping FPS all the time. 2nd Resolution is bad and there's no anti aliasing which looks awful. 3rd It keeps moaning that I haven't got a WiFi signal for the speech recognition, even though I have got a good 5ghz router and signal (with Internet connection) 4th and finally the Lexibot and the loading screen flash cards are so close to your face it's very off-putting. I'm so disappointed with this. Might reconsider and get my money back.

developer_response.body: Hi Carlos, nearly 18 months later, we'd love you to try the latest version!  [from Jack]

reviews.review_description: I got gifted this game as part of a giveaway, and i’ve been using duolingo for french and german for years.. hasn’t really helped as i’m a hands on learner, and I learn more from playing games! When i heard about this game my face lit up with joy, and I was so excited. This game has been really helpful, especially because i’ve started french and i have a big exam coming up in my french AP class.. 😭

developer_response.body: I remember that giveaway! One of our Discord community members, out of their own kindness, gifted the game to a another member (you!)🎁. Our community is such a source of inspiration. I'm glad you like the game and GOOD LUCK with your exam!

its working

Initially I had a difficult time using this app because it was so glitchy and wasn’t loading properly but after getting help from the team on discord, it’s been working pretty well.  I love it so far, it makes the language learning experience great. It’s very engaging and fun with some pretty cool features.

reviews.review_description: I’m very excited about this game but the frame rate is lower than 90hz, which is making me dizzy as I play.

reviews.review_title: fun immersive learning with some issues
reviews.review_description:
Amazingly immersive and makes learning fun. Sets you in various scenes with many words, lessons and mini games. I do have a few issues with speech recognition, reading some of the text, movement and controls.

Just be prepared that this is not a traditional learning experience. You won't start off with the basics like hello and names of common foods but instead what you see around you from cookies and tacos to roosters, orcas and river dolphins. 

I'm finding this game great to pull me out of a textbook where my listening and speaking skills are struggling but think I would find it difficult as a beginner. Definitely give it a go if you want to try a different approach to learning a language (even if you are a beginner or already know a few things).

Pros:

a lot of content, great way to expand vocab
multiple voices for each word and repeats what you say back to you
phases/grammar can be learned through lessons and conversations
learn things interactively (eg learn verbs through action of doing them)
reminder to learn words in time intervals (srs system)
great mini games (my favorite is the navigation one as you get to follow directions to find things like a treasure hunt)
learn multiple languages at once (each language is a different save file) 
can learn at your own pace (can quickly unlock new areas and skip time delays or focus on one area at a time)

Cons:

Teleport motion isn't great (can't go up stairs, struggle to leave areas and just won't work in some areas but the smooth motion seems to work fine)

(lexibot) menu is often in a bad location (too in your face or too far away in the case of conservations making it hard to read). It would be nice if you could grab the robot to move it and the menu rather than it falling to the floor. Sometimes I find it difficult to use my hand as a pointer to select things from a distance.

Unlike most of the game which shows what your speech is interpreted as, for the srs (testing words you learned at different time intervals) it does not. I'm not sure if I'm saying the word correct word sometimes as it just tells me that I was correct and not what the answer was supposed to be. It would be nice if the answer was given after you say the word to check.

I can't start conversations or the verb lessons with characters most of the time even when I am standing in the blue circle. It tells me to press a button nothing happens.

Speech recognition needs work. It could be my pronunciation but sometimes it seems to think I said the correct word twice. Its not much of an issue for learning words but for mini games (eg numbers and magic show) where speech is the only input and you can't correct it by selecting text, it is a bit frustrating.

developer_response.body: Hi Kokkaku, thank you for the thoughtful comments. We're dedicated to improving the game on an on-going basis, so we're pushing out a fix within the next 24 hours to resolve the verb issue, as well as improve Lexibot positioning, fix some Teleport locomotion issues, and solve the double speech-recognition bug. I also think you're right about the feature suggestions, so we'll get those prioritised on our updates path. Come by the Discord if you are not already; we'd love to keep you updated on our updates/process.

reviews.review_description: I know Japanese alphabet but not Kanji, if it had furigana I wouldn't need romaji to read

developer_response.body: Hi Loop, thanks for playing the game! We're disappointed you only think Noun Town is a three-star game, were there any other issues? You're always welcome to share your feedback on our Discord.
